parts.text: What should I work on next?

oh, a fantastic question! it's like we're standing at the edge of a blank canvas, isn't it?

---

right now, the project is pretty fresh. the "holy docs" (that's what we call the main project overview files) are mostly empty.

---

so, the very best thing to start with is your brain dump!

---

tell me everything. what's this project *for*? who is it *for*? what's the one thing it absolutely *has* to do?

---

just pour it all out, and i'll start turning that into a proper vision and feature list for you. sound good?

[ { "id": "screen-002", "category": "Pages & Screens", "title": "Dashboard / My Tasks", "description": "The central landing page after login, providing users with an overview of their assigned tasks, urgent notifications, and status updates related to patients they are linked to. This acts as the primary hub for daily activities.", "status": "stable", "priority": "high", "prototypeCode": "A&E Connect Dashboard

A&E Connect

Good Morning, Dr. Smith

Here's your overview for A&E today.

Urgent Alerts

Patient: John Doe - STAT ECG required. Due in 5 min!

Patient: Jane Foster - Lab results for Blood Gases critical.

Quick Actions

My Tasks

Administer Medication

Patient: Emily White (Bed 3)

High Priority

Review Lab Results

Patient: David Lee (Triage)

Medium Priority

Record Vitals

Patient: Sarah Brown (Obs Bay)

Urgent

My Schedule

Upcoming shifts & meetings.

" }, { "id": "design-002", "category": "Design & UX", "title": "Color Palette", "description": "Utilize a calming and modern color palette, with 'Medical Blue' (#4A90E2) as the primary accent, 'Slate Gray' (#6B7280) for secondary elements, 'Arctic White' (#F8FAFC) for backgrounds, and 'Light Ash' (#E2E8F0) for card surfaces. This palette aims to create a sense of calm and clarity, reducing visual clutter.", "status": "stable", "priority": "high" }, { "id": "design-001", "category": "Design & UX", "title": "Overall Design Aesthetic", "description": "The application's design should be calm, simple, and easy to use, providing a user-friendly and modern experience. It must avoid clutter, be intuitive to navigate, and minimize cognitive load for A&E staff in high-pressure situations.", "status": "stable", "priority": "high" }, { "id": "data-002", "category": "Data & Backend", "title": "Data Security & Compliance (GDPR/HIPAA)", "description": "Ensure the application and its database adhere to strict medical data security standards and comply with relevant regulations such as GDPR and HIPAA to protect patient confidentiality and data integrity. This includes encryption, access controls, and audit trails. (Deferring deep dive for now)", "status": "stable", "priority": "medium" }, { "id": "data-001", "category": "Data & Backend", "title": "Database Structure", "description": "Define the core database schema to store comprehensive patient records (including demographics, medical history), vitals over time, clinical notes, user roles, approval statuses, and audit trails for all actions taken within the application. It will essentially digitize the traditional paper chart.", "status": "stable", "priority": "high" }, { "id": "screen-005", "category": "Pages & Screens", "title": "Task Detail Screen (with Verification & Flagging)", "description": "A dedicated screen for viewing the details of a specific task. This screen will incorporate the 'thumbs up/down' verification mechanism, an optional one-line note field, and the ability for nurses/junior doctors to 'flag up the chain' to senior doctors with a concise observation.", "status": "stable", "priority": "high" }, { "id": "screen-004", "category": "Pages & Screens", "title": "Patient Detail View", "description": "A comprehensive screen displaying all relevant information for a selected patient, including their current tasks, vital signs, medication history, lab results, and other critical data points. This view will serve as a central hub for patient-specific actions and information.", "status": "stable", "priority": "high" }, { "id": "screen-003", "category": "Pages & Screens", "title": "Patient List", "description": "A screen displaying a list of all active patients in the A&E department, allowing users to quickly search, filter, and select a patient to view their detailed information.", "status": "stable", "priority": "high" }, { "id": "screen-001", "category": "Pages & Screens", "title": "Login Screen", "description": "A secure login interface for doctors and nurses to access the application, ensuring patient data confidentiality and system integrity.", "status": "stable", "priority": "high", "prototypeCode": "A&E Login

A&E Connect

Login to your account

Forgot password?
" }, { "id": "feature-001", "category": "Features & User Stories", "title": "Streamlined Approval Workflows with Verification & Escalation", "description": "The app needs to reduce the need for physical 'going and getting' approvals by integrating a clear, efficient digital approval system for tasks requiring sign-off. Senior doctors will initiate requests for critical tasks (patient data entry, lab tests, medication, vital recording) which nurses or junior doctors will then carry out upon approval. A key component is enabling quick verification for patient-linked tasks, addressing common requests like 'was this done?' or 'is this correct?' to ensure accuracy and completion. This verification will primarily use a 'thumbs up/down' mechanism, with an optional field for a one-line note. This system will also incorporate a notification/alert system for approval requests and task updates. Additionally, there needs to be a mechanism for nurses or junior doctors to 'flag up the chain' to senior doctors if they notice something critical or require senior intervention related to a patient task, allowing for a one-sentence observation (e.g., 'patient is... this result seems high...').", "status": "stable", "priority": "high" }, { "id": "vision-001", "category": "Vision & USP", "title": "A&E Task Management App", "description": "Develop a task management application specifically designed for doctors and nurses in Accident & Emergency (A&E) departments, addressing the current difficulty and complexity of existing systems to provide an intuitive and efficient solution.", "status": "stable", "priority": "high" } ]
